title: neolloydia pilispina
intro: Neolloydia pilispina, also known as the "hairy-stemmed cactus," is a type of succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat various conditions, including wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Neolloydia pilispina can be made by infusing the plant's stems in water or by using it as a topical cream or ointment.
